content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the prompt-contract changes and found two issues: canonical-marker validation is inconsistent with the detection logic used by downstream consumers, and the new placeholder requirement breaks existing template overrides at runtime.

## Amendments

1. src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:104-129 and 173-180, plus src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:320-326 and src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:385-394. `prompt_uses_contract()` only recognizes the canonical prompt when the contract marker appears before the first canonical sections, but `validate_canonical_prompt_shape()` only checks that the marker exists somewhere at top level. That means a refiner or prompt override can move `<!-- ralph-task-prompt-contract: bead_execution_prompt/1 -->` to the bottom of the prompt, still pass validation, and then lose all downstream contract guidance because later consumers call `prompt_uses_contract()` and return an empty `task_prompt_contract` block. The validator needs to enforce the same marker-placement rule that detection and downstream consumers rely on.
   Rationale: Prompt review can currently approve a prompt as contract-preserving even though later stages stop treating it as canonical.
2. src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:406-458 and 750-756, plus src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:109-165 and src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:73-86. The new `task_prompt_contract` placeholder was made mandatory for every stage/prompt-review/completion/final-review override template. Existing custom overrides that predate this change will now fail template validation with `missing required placeholder`, even though callers already provide the new value and those overrides could otherwise keep rendering exactly as before. This is a runtime compatibility regression outside the prompt-contract behavior itself.
   Rationale: Any workspace or project with older template overrides will lose the ability to run stages after upgrading to this change.
